items.headline: Historic house preservation permit for four new townhomes project
items.summary: A resource alteration permit at 434 Crescent Avenue would allow development of four three-story townhomes on the rear of the property while retaining the existing 960-square-foot historic single-family house. The Heritage Preservation Commission is reviewing whether the project adequately preserves the historic resource.

items.headline: City Council Approves New Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel
items.summary: San José amended its contract with Clear Channel Outdoor LLC for large format digital signage on city-owned property. This affects what advertising and messaging residents will see on public spaces throughout the city.

items.headline: City Issues RFP for Encampment Cleanup Services
items.summary: The City Council reviewed proposals for contracted encampment cleanup services, indicating a potential shift in how San José manages homelessness-related site cleanups and sanitation.

items.headline: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The City Council took action on the Terminal Elevator Replacement Project, suggesting infrastructure upgrades to a key city facility that residents or visitors use.

items.headline: City negotiating land deal with school district on Finch Avenue
items.summary: Cupertino is in closed-door negotiations with Cupertino Union School District over the purchase of property at 10480 Finch Avenue, with price and payment terms still being worked out. This could signal a potential acquisition for school or city use, though details remain confidential under state law.

items.headline: Milpitas adopts Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan
items.summary: The city council approved a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District, which will guide development of green spaces and recreational connections in this key area of Milpitas. This plan will shape how residents can access outdoor amenities in a rapidly developing part of the city.

items.headline: Major 260-unit apartment complex breaks ground in Berryessa
items.summary: Berryessa Family Apartments, a $52.4M new construction project, will add 260 rental units to the Berryessa neighborhood. This represents significant new housing supply in an area that has seen limited multifamily development.

items.headline: Arc'Teryx flagship retail opens on Stevens Creek Boulevard
items.summary: The outdoor gear company Arc'Teryx is opening a $1.7M retail location at Stevens Creek Boulevard. This marks a notable retail expansion bringing a premium international brand to San José's shopping corridor.
